criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose inferior myocardial infarction, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment depression in leads II, III, and aVF
Presence of ST-segment elevation in leads V1 and V2
Presence of ST-segment elevation in inferior leads
Presence of diffuse concave ST-segment elevation in limb leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment elevation in inferior leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST elevation in at least two of the inferior leads, including leads II, III, and aVF?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show ST elevation? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complexes with ST elevation? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have inferior myocardial infarction?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
